channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
item.description: American Airlines is planning to install SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi in hundreds of its airplanes, the airline announced today. American says the deployment will start in the first quarter of 2027,...

item.title: Token-level eval harness for tool-calling agents: what we wired up
item.description: TL;DR: We replaced our "did the agent finish the task" pass/fail eval with a token-level harness that scores tool selection, argument shape, and recovery behavior separately. Pass rate went from a...

item.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
item.description: Human Archive, a startup founded by Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training...

item.title: Trust Boundaries in Client-Side Health Apps
item.description: Trust-boundary-aligned resource set: chronic pain tracking resources If you came here from the failure-mode and testing path, this is the boundary question underneath it. Read first: Service...

item.title: Why Some Codebases Are Hard to Understand: Cognitive Surface Area and the Hidden Cost of System Navigation
item.description: Information Foraging: How Developers Navigate Systems Developers rarely read code from top to bottom. They navigate it. When debugging or extending a system, developers follow cues that suggest...

item.title: Memory V recreates the Memorymoog without the massive headaches or price tag
item.description: The Memorymoog is legendary for being an absolutely massive-sounding synth and being incredibly unreliable. But now you can enjoy its classic Moog sound without the headaches or the sky-high...

item.title: The fastest way to update Node.js on your Mac
item.description: Posted on Jul 22, 2023 1 min read 🤖 AI summary: This article outlines a quick method for updating Node.js on macOS by utilizing the n package. After verifying your current version, you can install...

item.title: Prompt is Not Runtime: Why I Rejected LLM State-Machines for Deterministic FinTech
item.description: Prompts are Heuristics, Not Code There is an excellent article trending on Hacker News right now titled "Prompt Is Not Runtime." It targets a major architectural delusion of the current AI-hype...

item.title: SDD en proyectos brownfield: pros, contras y la estrategia que realmente funciona

item.title: Hexagonal Architecture in Practice: Ports, Adapters, and Tests That Skip the Database
item.description: This is the technical companion to a piece I wrote about cutting our CI from ~20 minutes to ~5. That post is the story; this one is the receipts. If you are about to do the same migration in a...

item.title: Your Playwright Tests Will Need Refactoring. Here's How to Make It Painless
item.description: You write 50 tests. Everything works. Six months later the team grows, tests become 300, and someone changes a constructor — and you spend two days updating imports across the entire project....

item.title: Development of a custom API layer for Framer CMS integration
item.description: As part of the continuous evolution of the digital services, we developed a custom Events & News management and presentation platform connected with Framer CMS through an intermediate API Layer....

item.title: Stream 24/7 on YouTube with Ant Media Server
item.description: Posted on Jan 22, 2022 5 min read 🤖 AI summary: This tutorial walks you through setting up Ant Media Server on a Linux VPS to enable 24/7 live streaming to YouTube. Ant Media is an open-source...

item.title: Saving for a Switch 2 is easier with Newegg’s gift card deal
item.description: If you foresee a bunch of Nintendo purchases in your future, Newegg’s deal on Nintendo gift cards might just help save your wallet. The retailer is offering a deal that lets gamers get $200 worth...

item.title: Oppo’s Bubble is a thin round screen for taking rear camera selfies
item.description: Oppo launched a new smartphone accessory that makes it easier to snap selfies using your smartphone's rear cameras that typically feature better sensors than front-facing cameras. The Bubble...

item.title: Govee included a book on ‘White Supremacy’ in its website imagery
item.description: Companies often include background props in their product imagery that serve as set dressing, but one of Govee's decorative choices is raising some eyebrows. An eagle-eyed Verge reader spotted...

item.title: Identifying People Using Wi-Fi Routers
item.description: Not identifying people based on their use of Wi-Fi routers, but identifying people using Wi-Fi signals. This is accomplished through what is known as WiFi sensing, or the use of WiFi signals to...

item.title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
item.description: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies

item.title: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
item.description: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution. Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next...

item.title: Jony Ive’s Ferrari looks nothing like a Ferrari
item.description: Ferrari makes some of the fastest cars on the planet, so it's anyone's guess how the Italian automaker ended up being so incredibly late to the EV party. Long after most automakers have...

item.title: Nvidia has retired its GeForce Control Panel app after 20 years
item.description: Nvidia announced more than two years ago that it was working to replace its Control Panel app on Windows with a new Nvidia app. After porting across various features to the Nvidia app, Nvidia is...

item.title: How clips ate the internet
item.description: Once upon a time, you could probably guess why most things appeared on your feed. Maybe you followed the creator who posted it; maybe you'd liked their stuff in the past; maybe all your friends...

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
item.description: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.

item.title: Sundar Pichai on AI, the future of search, and what’s happening to the web

item.title: Nobody wants to tell me why they only listen to their own Suno slop
item.description: There's this alarming trend in the Suno subreddit. People aren't just prompting AI songs; they're sitting around listening almost exclusively to their own slop. And in some cases, they proudly...

item.title: The Download: puncturing the AI jobs panic
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria Despite the...

item.title: [THN Webinar] New AI DDoS Attacks Are Smarter. Learn How to Fight Back
item.description: Every single day, hackers are finding new ways to crash websites and steal data. But right now, something has changed. Hackers are no longer working alone. They are now using powerful Artificial...

item.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out updates to fix a remote code execution vulnerability impacting SharePoint that could be exploited by bad actors in attacks without requiring any specialized conditions to...

item.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
item.description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) was supposed to close a critical gap in identity security. It meant that, even if an attacker possessed the account credentials, they couldn't log in without the...

item.title: Quiz: Will AI Destroy Your Career?
item.description: Some jobs may be toast. Some will survive. Click your answers to learn your fate.
